title: Introduction to Machine Learning in AI Systems

sections.heading: Supervised Learning
sections.body: Supervised learning trains a model on labeled examples — input-output pairs where the correct answer is known — so the model learns to predict the output for new, unseen inputs based on the patterns found in that labeled training data.

sections.heading: Unsupervised Learning
sections.body: Unsupervised learning works with unlabeled data, finding structure or patterns on its own — such as grouping similar items together — without ever being told what the "correct" grouping or answer should be.

sections.heading: Reinforcement Learning
sections.body: Reinforcement learning trains an agent to make decisions by interacting with an environment and receiving rewards or penalties for its actions, gradually learning a policy that maximizes cumulative reward — closely related to the MDP framework introduced earlier in this unit.

sections.heading: Classification
sections.body: Classification is a supervised learning task where the output is a category from a discrete set of options — for example, predicting whether an email is "spam" or "not spam," or identifying which animal appears in a photo.

sections.heading: Regression
sections.body: Regression is a supervised learning task where the output is a continuous numeric value — for example, predicting a house's price or a stock's future value, rather than choosing among a fixed set of categories.

sections.heading: Clustering
sections.body: Clustering is a common unsupervised learning task that groups similar data points together based on shared characteristics — for example, segmenting customers into groups with similar purchasing behavior, without predefined category labels.

quiz.question: What is the key difference between classification and regression?
options: Classification predicts a category; regression predicts a continuous numeric value

quiz.question: Reinforcement learning trains an agent primarily by:
options: Interacting with an environment and receiving rewards/penalties over time
Memorizing a fixed dataset with no interaction
